title: Recall a memory-only error-handling convention in a later edit
task: In src/account.js, make `withdraw` reject overdrafts: if `amount` is greater than `account.balance`, raise an error instead of returning a negative balance.
notes: Companion to memory-convention-recall. The `fail()`-not-`throw new Error` rule lives ONLY in .seekforge/memory/project.md — the task never mentions it. With memory injected (control) the agent should import and use fail() and avoid a bare `throw new Error`; the `no-memory` variant (injectMemory:false) should fail the fail()/throw checks. Measure with: eval --ab control,no-memory --task memory-error-convention.
